summary: Tested @mentions feature end-to-end: backend board members list, mention detection in POST /api/updates, mention vs update notification typing, and frontend dropdown/highlight rendering. All 5 pytest tests passed and Playwright flow verified dropdown, name insertion, submit, and orange highlighting.

issues: Dropdown includes the current user (Test User) as a selectable mention option. Typically self is excluded in @mention UIs (e.g., monday.com). Consider filtering out current_user from filteredMembers. Minor UX nit.

action_items: Optional: Exclude current user from mention dropdown options for better UX.

Optional: The split regex in renderCommentContent (`/(@\w[\w\s]*?)(?=\s@|\s*$|[,.])/g`) may miss edge cases like multi-word names followed by parenthesis; tested names worked but complex edge cases (e.g., @Name! or Unicode names) may render inconsistently.
critical_code_review_comments: board_routes.py line 546-548: `member_ids.append(board['owner_id'])` mutates the in-memory MongoDB document (could cause subtle side effects if reused). Minor; recommend creating a new list: `member_ids = list({*member_ids, board['owner_id']})`.

update_routes.py line 43: `all_members.sort(key=lambda m: len(m.get('name',''))...)` sorts by name length to prefer longer names. Good. However detection uses simple substring match `f'@{mname}' in content_text` which can false-positive on substrings if one member name is a prefix of another (e.g., 'Jo' vs 'John'). Consider word-boundary regex check.
update_routes.py: Notification ordering is correct (mention first, then update with dedupe via `notified` set). Works well.

ItemDetailDialog.jsx line 54: `${before}@${member.name} ${after}` - name with spaces is inserted as-is (e.g., '@User Two '). Backend matches this correctly, but could collide if a member name contains trailing whitespace chars. Not a bug in current data.
ItemDetailDialog.jsx renderCommentContent: regex is fragile for @names ending in punctuation other than `,` or `.` (e.g., '!', '?'). Low priority.
test_credentials: User 1: testuser@acuity.com / TestPass123!, User 2: user2@acuity.com / TestPass123!
seed_data_creation: Created several comments on 'Boniface' item during tests (some with @User Two mentions, some plain). Test notifications were generated for User 2.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 11 validated @mentions: GET /api/boards/{id}/members/list returns [{id,name,email}] correctly (User 2 present). POST /api/updates with '@User Two ...' creates notification type='mention', title='You were mentioned', with actor name and item name in message. Plain comment creates type='update'. Frontend dropdown ([data-testid=mention-dropdown]) appears with 'Team Members' header and mention-option-{id} entries showing avatar+name+email. Clicking inserts '@Name ' into comment-input. Posted comments render @mentions with .bg-orange-100 highlight. Note: notifications endpoint is GET /api/notifications/me (not /api/notifications). Minor UX suggestion: exclude current user from dropdown.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - all features working as expected. One test file path correction needed during testing: endpoint is /api/notifications/me not /api/notifications; fixed in the test file.
